sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Newcastle United have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins but suffering 2 losses, with strong attacking output as seen in their 4-1 victory over Everton. Tottenham Hotspur, however, are in poor form, losing 3 of their last 5 matches, including a 4-1 defeat to Arsenal, and failing to win in their last 4 games. This gives Newcastle a clear edge heading into the match at St James' Park.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Newcastle have dominated Tottenham, winning 2-0 recently and drawing 1-1 earlier in the season. Over the last 5 head-to-head matches, Newcastle remain unbeaten with 4 wins and 1 draw, indicating a psychological advantage over their opponents.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Both teams are closely matched in the league table, with Newcastle at 13th and Tottenham at 12th, each on 18 points. Newcastle's defensive solidity (5 clean sheets, 7th least goals conceded) contrasts with Tottenham's struggles to keep clean sheets (14 consecutive H2H games without one), while both sides show attacking intent with high shot volumes and big chances created.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Newcastle, Harvey Barnes (4 goals in last 5 matches) and Malick Thiaw (7.66 avg rating) are in top form, while Bruno Guimarães adds midfield creativity. Tottenham will rely on Richarlison (5 goals this season) and Mohammed Kudus (4 assists) to turn their fortunes around, but their defensive frailties could be exploited.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Newcastle's ability to score early (first to score in 9/9 recent games) and dominate first halves (4/5 first-half winners) could put Tottenham on the back foot, especially given Spurs' tendency to concede first (5/7 games). Tottenham's high card count (5/7 games over 4.5 cards) suggests a physical battle, while Newcastle's aerial strength (5th in aerial duels won) could be decisive.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Newcastle's superior recent form, unbeaten head-to-head record, and home advantage, they are favored to win with a 41% probability per Sofascore. Tottenham's defensive issues and lack of wins in recent games (0/4) make an away victory (34%) less likely, though a draw (25%) remains possible. Expect a narrow Newcastle win in a competitive encounter.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline remains level with no goals scored by either Newcastle United or Tottenham Hotspur. The game has been competitive, with Newcastle showing more control in terms of possession and territorial dominance. Both teams have had their moments, but neither has managed to convert their opportunities into goals. Newcastle's defense has looked solid, while Tottenham has shown glimpses of threat on the counter but lacked precision in the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Newcastle United's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Newcastle United have been the more dominant side in the first half, controlling the ball for significant periods and creating pressure through frequent entries into the final third and penalty area. They've won a higher percentage of duels, particularly in the air, showcasing physical superiority. Despite not creating clear-cut chances, their intent has been evident with multiple shots on target and hitting the woodwork once. Their midfield, led by players like Sandro Tonali and Lewis Miley, has been instrumental in maintaining this control.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tottenham Hotspur's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Tottenham Hotspur have struggled to assert themselves for much of the first half, with less possession and fewer entries into dangerous areas. They did manage to create one notable chance, but the miss highlights their difficulty in finishing. Defensively, they've held firm under pressure, with players like Cristian Romero and Kevin Danso contributing to key clearances. However, their inability to win duels consistently has allowed Newcastle to dictate the tempo for large parts of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The attack momentum in the first half started with Tottenham showing early initiative, pushing forward in the opening minutes. However, Newcastle quickly took over, maintaining a strong attacking presence for a prolonged period with consistent spells of pressure. Their dominance was particularly evident through the middle of the half as they repeatedly threatened Tottenham's defense. Towards the end of the half, Tottenham fought back with a notable shift in momentum, mounting a series of attacking moves to close out the half on a more positive note.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch in the second half
halftimeSections.text: In the second half, Newcastle will likely look to capitalize on their first-half dominance by converting territorial advantage into goals. Players like Lewis Miley and Jacob Murphy, who have shown creativity, could be key in unlocking Tottenham's defense. For Tottenham, improving their final third efficiency and leveraging players like Mohammed Kudus, who created a big chance, will be crucial. Discipline will also be important for Spurs, as they’ve already picked up a yellow card and face a physically imposing Newcastle side.
